title: Versatile Loose Batter
excerpt: Ingredients 125 ml coconut milk 125 ml sunflower oil 1 large egg salt to taste 5 ml turmeric 5 ml coarsely crushed cumin seeds 65 ml finely chopped coriander leaves 250 ml self-raising flour Method Whisk the coconut milk, oil and egg together. Season to taste with salt and whisk in the rest of the ingredients. Pour slowly …
html:
Ingredients

  • 125 ml coconut milk
  • 125 ml sunflower oil
  • 1 large egg
  • salt to taste
  • 5 ml turmeric
  • 5 ml coarsely crushed cumin seeds
  • 65 ml finely chopped coriander leaves
  • 250 ml self-raising flour

Method

Whisk the coconut milk, oil and egg together.

Season to taste with salt and whisk in the rest of the ingredients.

Pour slowly over about 1.2 kg cooked curried mince in an ovenproof dish.

Bake at 180°C for 30 minutes or until cooked and golden brown.

Serves 4 – 6 portions together with sambals and toasted garlic rotis.

Easy!

Tip: You can use full-cream milk and add a bit of sugar, cinnamon or any other sweet spice to the mixture and then pour it over fruit for a lovely pudding. Or, you can add crushed garlic, chilli flakes, curry powder, finely chopped coriander leaves and even a fruity chutney to the mixture for a Cape Malay flavour. In that case, replace the milk with coconut milk. And of course, for a bigger bake, you can double everything.
